current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

caution_notes: Warm water may increase trout stress.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing is tough with very warm water at 76.6°F, challenging trout activity. Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs to help target the fish.
generated_text.best_window: Day 1 morning is the best window because lower light and moderate sky cover provide balanced conditions that favor trout positioning in runs, seams, and structure as indicated by the angler analysis.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is 76.6°F, flow is 109 cfs, gage height is 3.78 ft, and turbidity measures 8.8 NTU at the Etowah River USGS site.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover averages 19.36%, pressure is steady at 30.05 inHg with no strong trend, wind is from the west at 4.5 mph with gusts up to 16.1 mph, and rain probability is low at 17%.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will seek cold-water refuges and oxygenated water due to warm temperatures, positioning along runs, seams, and structure. Prioritize clean drifts and smaller natural patterns like egg patterns and squirmy worms; use attractor nymphs like Duracell cautiously. Warm water increases trout stress, so consider not targeting trout aggressively.
